subject: Biology
title: Plants and Seasonal Change
description: A fast breakdown of Plants and Seasonal Change for builders.

hook.text: Plants track the seasons by measuring day length—a process called photoperiodism.

buildup.text: Short-day plants like chrysanthemums flower when nights grow long. Long-day plants flower in summer.

discovery.text: A pigment called phytochrome detects the ratio of red to far-red light, signaling day length.

twist.text: Deciduous trees drop their leaves to survive winter—frozen water in leaves would kill the cells.

climax.text: Climate change is shifting bloom times, sometimes causing plants and pollinators to fall out of sync.

punchline.text: Plants read the calendar with light, not dates.

quiz.question: How do plants measure day length?
